turns.user_message: I just watched the little loading circle spin on my screen for ten straight minutes without even trying to click anything. It’s funny because a few months ago I would have just refreshed the page and kept pushing through without a second thought. Now I just sit there feeling completely hollow while the progress bar crawls forward. I know I should probably just close the tab and walk away for a bit, but my hands won't even move. It’s like my brain just hit a wall and decided to stay there.

That same spinning icon has been showing up every single morning lately, and I can feel my patience completely draining away before the day even starts. I keep telling myself it is just a temporary glitch, but the dread settles in my chest the moment I see it. I used to just laugh it off and grab another coffee, but now I just stare at the desk feeling completely disconnected from everything around me. Every little delay feels like a heavy weight pressing down on my shoulders. I just want to push through it, but the resistance is getting harder to ignore each time.

I finally snapped when that same loading circle appeared again yesterday, and I just sat there watching it spin until my vision actually blurred. It used to be a harmless little pause that I barely noticed, but now it feels like a physical barrier keeping me trapped in this endless loop. I try to force myself to focus, but my mind just goes completely blank and I feel utterly drained. The sheer exhaustion makes it impossible to care about anything else, and I just sit there paralyzed while the hours slip away. I am so tired of fighting against my own brain just to get through a simple task.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment. This used to not bother the speaker.
